questions.question: What is a Stack?
options: A) A linear data structure
B) A data structure that follows the Last In First Out (LIFO) principle
C) A data structure that follows the First In First Out (FIFO) principle
D) A data structure that follows the Random Access principle
questions.explanation: A stack is a linear data structure that follows the Last In First Out (LIFO) principle, meaning the last element added to the stack will be the first one to be removed.

questions.question: What is the Pop operation in a Stack?
options: A) To add an element to the stack
B) To remove the top element from the stack
C) To insert an element at a specific position in the stack
D) To delete an element from the stack
questions.explanation: The Pop operation in a Stack is to remove the top element from the stack.

questions.question: How can you implement a Stack using an Array in programming?
options: A) By using the index of the element to access and modify
B) By using a pointer to a single element in the array
C) By using two pointers, one for the top element and one for the next available element
D) By using a linked list as the data structure
questions.explanation: A Stack can be implemented using an Array by using two pointers, one for the top element and one for the next available element.

questions.question: What is a Common application of Stacks?
options: A) Database management
B) File system management
C) Parsing expressions in compilers and interpreters
D) All the above
questions.explanation: Stacks are commonly used in parsing expressions in compilers and interpreters, as well as in database management and file system management.

questions.question: Which Stack operation is used to add an element to the top of the stack?
options: A) Push
B) Pop
C) Insert
D) Delete
questions.explanation: The Push operation is used to add an element to the top of the stack.
